segments.title: Yokosuka Peace Park and its naval-heritage landscape
segments.summary: From Yokosuka Peace Park, the group looks across a city whose museum, harbour landmarks, and visible naval heritage reward unhurried exploration.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke introduces Yokosuka Peace Park, which overlooks the Yokosuka City Museum and the surrounding waterfront. He and his companion describe the municipal museum as unexpectedly extensive, with several floors and a substantial amount to see. Looking out from the park, they identify the wider maritime setting and discuss the likely position of Mikasa's mast. The exchange presents Yokosuka as a place where urban geography, museums, and naval heritage can be read together rather than visited as isolated attractions. It is a useful watch point for visitors planning time around the city's historical waterfront.

evidence.note: They introduce Peace Park, describe the Yokosuka City Museum, and orient themselves to the waterfront and Mikasa.
